task_description:
Screen each abstract for relevance to machine learning for medical imaging diagnosis, and rate methodological quality.

Relevance: RELEVANT if the paper applies ML or deep learning to classify, detect, or grade a medical condition from any imaging modality (radiology, pathology, fundus, dermatology, etc). NOT_RELEVANT if the ML task is non-diagnostic (segmentation only, registration, reconstruction) or the domain is non-medical.

Quality rubric:
  1 = No methodology described or purely conceptual.
  2 = Approach mentioned but no evaluation metrics or baselines reported.
  3 = Evaluation metrics and dataset described; limited or no baseline comparison.
  4 = Full methodology with multiple baselines, ablation, and reproducible evaluation.

papers.title: Towards AI-Assisted Radiology: Opportunities and Limitations
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence is poised to transform radiology practice. This perspective discusses potential applications in chest X-ray triage, CT stroke detection, and mammography screening. We outline key challenges including dataset bias, regulatory hurdles, and radiologist workflow integration. No empirical model is trained or evaluated; this is a conceptual overview.
papers.methodology_hint: perspective article, no model, no evaluation, no metrics
papers.claimed_contribution: Perspective on AI opportunities and limitations in radiology practice.

papers.title: ResNet-50 for Mammography Mass Detection with CBIS-DDSM Benchmark
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a ResNet-50 on the CBIS-DDSM mammography dataset to detect malignant and benign masses. AUC 0.88 is achieved on the official test partition. We compare against VGG-16 and an SVM on handcrafted GLCM features. An ablation study shows that data augmentation with synthetic deformation contributes 3.2 points to AUC. Reproducibility scripts are released on GitHub.
papers.topic_hint: ResNet-50, mammography, CBIS-DDSM, mass detection, AUC
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, CBIS-DDSM, AUC 0.88, VGG and SVM baselines, ablation, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 mammography mass detection AUC 0.88, ablation, two baselines, reproducible.

papers.title: Transformer Architecture for 3D Brain Tumour Segmentation
papers.abstract: We develop a Swin Transformer variant for volumetric brain tumour segmentation on BraTS 2021. Dice 0.92 for whole tumour, 0.88 for tumour core, and 0.85 for enhancing tumour are achieved. This is a segmentation method; the model produces voxel-level masks, not disease diagnosis or grading labels.
papers.topic_hint: Swin Transformer, brain tumour, segmentation, BraTS 2021
papers.methodology_hint: Swin Transformer, BraTS 2021, Dice 0.92/0.88/0.85 — segmentation not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Swin Transformer brain tumour segmentation, Dice 0.92/0.88/0.85 on BraTS 2021.

papers.title: Deep Learning May Improve Chest X-Ray Interpretation
papers.abstract: Machine learning holds promise for automated chest radiograph analysis. We believe that deep learning architectures could improve detection of pneumonia, atelectasis, and pleural effusion if trained on sufficiently large and diverse datasets. Preliminary internal experiments suggest encouraging results. We plan future validation but present no quantitative results in this report.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, chest X-ray, pneumonia, preliminary
papers.methodology_hint: conceptual note, no metrics, no dataset details, no baseline, preliminary only
papers.claimed_contribution: Preliminary claim that deep learning may improve chest X-ray interpretation.

papers.title: EfficientNet-B4 for Multiclass Skin Lesion Diagnosis on ISIC 2019
papers.abstract: Automated skin lesion diagnosis from dermoscopy images can triage teledermatology workloads. We fine-tune EfficientNet-B4 on ISIC 2019 (25,000 images, 8 classes). Balanced accuracy 0.87 is achieved, outperforming the challenge winner. We compare against ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, and ViT-B baselines, provide class-wise calibration curves, and report performance stratified by skin tone using the ITA angle.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet-B4, dermoscopy, ISIC 2019, skin lesion diagnosis, balanced accuracy
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B4, ISIC 2019, balanced accuracy 0.87, 3 baselines, calibration, skin tone stratification
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet-B4 for 8-class skin lesion diagnosis, balanced accuracy 0.87, 3 baselines, skin tone analysis.

papers.title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in MRI via Reconstruction Error
papers.abstract: We train a variational autoencoder on 8,000 healthy brain MRI scans and use per-voxel reconstruction error as an anomaly score for detecting lesions in unseen pathological scans. AUC 0.79 averaged across four unseen pathology types is achieved without any labelled pathological training data. We do not classify specific disease categories; the approach flags anomalies rather than diagnosing conditions.
papers.topic_hint: VAE, anomaly detection, brain MRI, reconstruction error, unsupervised
papers.methodology_hint: VAE, healthy training only, AUC 0.79, 4 pathology types, anomaly detection — not diagnostic classification
papers.claimed_contribution: VAE anomaly detection AUC 0.79 across 4 pathologies without labelled training data.

papers.title: CNN for Diabetic Macular Oedema Grading from OCT with Uncertainty Estimation
papers.abstract: We train a MC-Dropout convolutional network on 12,000 OCT B-scans to grade diabetic macular oedema severity across three clinical levels. The model achieves AUC 0.93 and provides per-prediction confidence intervals. Uncertain predictions are flagged for human review in a hybrid human-AI workflow. We do not compare against other deep learning baselines but report performance against retinal specialist consensus.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, DMO grading, OCT, uncertainty, MC-Dropout
papers.methodology_hint: MC-Dropout CNN, 12k OCT, AUC 0.93, specialist consensus — no DL baseline comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: MC-Dropout CNN for DMO grading AUC 0.93 with uncertainty flagging, specialist comparison only.

papers.title: Physics-Based CT Image Reconstruction Using Deep Unrolling Networks
papers.abstract: We develop a deep unrolling algorithm that integrates physical forward models into iterative CT reconstruction, reducing noise artefacts at low radiation dose. SSIM improves from 0.79 to 0.91 versus filtered back-projection. The network reconstructs images; it does not classify pathology, detect disease, or produce diagnostic labels.
papers.topic_hint: deep unrolling, CT reconstruction, low-dose, image quality
papers.methodology_hint: deep unrolling, CT, SSIM 0.91, image reconstruction — no diagnostic task
papers.claimed_contribution: Deep unrolling CT reconstruction improving SSIM from 0.79 to 0.91, no disease classification.

papers.title: ViT-L for Colon Polyp Classification from Colonoscopy Images
papers.abstract: Accurate polyp classification supports informed resection decisions. We fine-tune ViT-L/16 on 41,000 colonoscopy images from two university hospitals to classify polyps into hyperplastic, sessile serrated, and adenomatous categories. Macro-AUC 0.92 is achieved. Comparisons against ResNet-101, DenseNet-121, and two gastroenterologist readers are provided. An ablation study evaluates the contribution of pre-training dataset scale and augmentation strategy.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, colonoscopy, colon polyp classification, macro-AUC, gastroenterology
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-L/16, 41k colonoscopy images, macro-AUC 0.92, 2 DL baselines, 2 gastroenterologist readers, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT-L colonoscopy polyp classification, macro-AUC 0.92, multiple baselines and ablation.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Network for Paired MRI-to-CT Synthesis
papers.abstract: We train a CycleGAN on paired MRI and CT volumes to synthesise pseudo-CT images for radiation treatment planning. Mean absolute error 67 HU is achieved on held-out patients. The model performs image-to-image translation; it does not classify disease, detect abnormalities, or produce diagnostic labels for any pathological condition.
papers.topic_hint: CycleGAN, MRI-to-CT synthesis, radiation planning, image translation
papers.methodology_hint: CycleGAN, paired MRI-CT, MAE 67 HU, image synthesis — no diagnostic task
papers.claimed_contribution: CycleGAN MRI-to-CT synthesis, MAE 67 HU, for radiation treatment planning, no diagnosis.
